html:
StructureItem

The Thulecite Wall is the toughest of the walls, having 500 health when initially placed and a maximum health of 800 when fully upgraded. It can be crafted using an Ancient Pseudoscience Station at the cost of one Thulecite. When broken down with a Hammer, it will yield Thulecite Fragments. A Thulecite Wall can be placed down to block the path of both creatures and the player. When built and placed, it will be at its third tier of repair. It can be upgraded into two more tiers with Thulecite Fragments or Thulecite. When hammered, the fifth tier will yield two Thulecite fragments, while the other tiers will only give one. This means that if the player doesn't repair a third tier wall and it gets damaged, it's better to hammer it down and replace it rather than repair it. Thulecite Walls are also found naturally in the Ruins, sometimes around Ancient Guardians and Damaged Clockworks.

Trivia

  • Before they were implemented, Thulecite Walls had a grayish color instead of the current golden one.
  • Since Thulecite Walls only take one Thulecite to build and each wall drops Thulecite Fragments when hammered, the player can make the walls, hammer them down when they get useless, and still craft the original Thulecite back as long as they place the walls separately or don't combine any of them. This also applies to stone walls, but only in multiples of six (6 stone is required to craft 6 stone walls).

Assets

  • Dropped
  • Phase 3
  • Phase 3 90°
  • Phase 2
  • Phase 2 90°
  • Phase 1
  • Phase 1 90°
  • Half Destroyed
  • Half Destroyed 90°
  • Destroyed
  • Destroyed 90°

Gallery

  • Old Thulecite Wall
  • Various states of repair of the Thulecite Wall.
  • Thulecite Walls as they were before the Stuff of Nightmares update.
  • All types of walls (not including DLC) built next to each other. In order, from outside to inside: Stone Wall, Thulecite Wall, Wood Wall, Hay Wall.

StructureItem
“A solid piece of history.”
–Wilson, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Will they fit in my pockets?”
–Willow, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Stack of old rocks!”
–Wolfgang, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Are the very stones haunted?”
–Wendy, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“POCKET STONER DEFENSE”
–WX-78, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“My pockets must be bigger on the inside.”
–Wickerbottom, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Heh, stoned again eh?”
–Woodie, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“An ancient wall.”
–Maxwell, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“A wall segment made from the endemic technology.”
–Wagstaff, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Önly the finest barriers för my fört.”
–Wigfrid, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Wow, these are really heavy.”
–Webber, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Ruins wall. Kinda at odds, no?”
–Walani, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“The stories these tell... fascinating...”
–Warly, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Ancient walls ta protect precious treasure.”
–Woodlegs, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“'TIS FOR WALL”
–Wilba, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“For the ground”
–Wormwood, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Better not drop this on my toe.”
–Wheeler, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“If I break it, I'll get to build it again.”
–Winona, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“It's of no use there on the ground.”
–Wortox, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“Will make strong wall!”
–Wurt, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“I like the spooky atmosphere.”
–Walter, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“It feels sturdy enough.”
–Wanda, Examining a thulecite wall item.
“An ancient piece of wall.”
–Wilson, Examining a thulecite wall.
“And they'll huff and they'll puff!”
–Willow, Examining a thulecite wall.
“Old wall is strong!”
–Wolfgang, Examining a thulecite wall.
“These didn't protect the ancients, now did they!”
–Wendy, Examining a thulecite wall.
“OLD BUT STILL FUNCTIONAL”
–WX-78, Examining a thulecite wall.
“A very secure wall.”
–Wickerbottom, Examining a thulecite wall.
“Crumbling, but still secure, eh?”
–Woodie, Examining a thulecite wall.
“An ancient wall.”
–Maxwell, Examining a thulecite wall.
“Nigh impenetrable.”
–Wigfrid, Examining a thulecite wall.
“Looks sturdy.”
–Webber, Examining a thulecite wall.
“Look at the carvings...”
–Warly, Examining a thulecite wall.
“Strong”
–Wormwood, Examining a thulecite wall.
“If I break it, I'll get to build it again.”
–Winona, Examining a thulecite wall.
“To keep you out, or keep me in?”
–Wortox, Examining a thulecite wall.
“How look so old already, flort?”
–Wurt, Examining a thulecite wall.
“I like the spooky atmosphere.”
–Walter, Examining a thulecite wall.
“New and old at the same time... did I just create a paradox?”
–Wanda, Examining a thulecite wall.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Tường Thulecite là bức tường cứng nhất, có 500 máu khi được đặt ban đầu và tối đa là 800 máu khi được nâng cấp hoàn toàn. Nó có thể được chế tạo bằng cách sử dụng Trạm Giả khoa học Cổ đại với chi phí là một Thulecite. Khi đập vỡ bằng Búa, nó sẽ thu được Mảnh Thulecite. Một Bức tường Thulecite có thể được đặt xuống để chặn đường đi của cả sinh vật và người chơi. Khi được xây dựng và lắp đặt, nó sẽ ở cấp độ sửa chữa thứ ba. Nó có thể được nâng cấp thành hai bậc nữa bằng Mảnh Thulecite hoặc Thulecite. Khi đập vào, tầng thứ năm sẽ tạo ra hai mảnh Thulecite, trong khi…

The '''Thulecite Wall''' is the toughest of the walls, having 500 health when initially placed and a maximum health of 800 when fully upgraded. It can be crafted using an [[Ancient Pseudoscience Station]] at the cost of one Thulecite. When broken down with a Hammer, it will yield [[Thulecite Fragments]]. A Thulecite Wall can be placed down to block the path of both creatures and the player. When built and placed, it will be at its third tier of repair. It can be upgraded into two more tiers with Thulecite Fragments or Thulecite. When hammered, the fifth tier will yield two Thulecite fragments, while the other tiers will only give one. This means that if the player doesn't repair a third tier wall and it gets damaged, it's better to hammer it down and replace it rather than repair it. Thulecite Walls are also found naturally in the [[Ruins]], sometimes around [[Ancient Guardian]]s and [[Broken Clockworks|Damaged Clockworks]].

=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
*Before they were implemented, Thulecite Walls had a grayish color instead of the current golden one.
*Since Thulecite Walls only take one Thulecite to build and each wall drops Thulecite Fragments when hammered, the player can make the walls, hammer them down when they get useless, and still craft the original Thulecite back as long as they place the walls separately or don't combine any of them. This also applies to stone walls, but only in multiples of six (6 stone is required to craft 6 stone walls).
